title: debug log level should dump out system call for container exec
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

This is a minor feature request to follow up with #6149.  One difficulty in debugging #6149 was the lack of diagnostic information.  When debug is turned on, podman should dump out the system call that was used to execute runc/crun and the parameters and data send to the container.  This will make it easier to debug problems

This should be similar to the data that is logged with conmon and the containerized networking.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.  Run with debug level = true

2. There is plenty of logging information to set up networking but no debug information on how the container was executed.

3.

**Describe the results you received:**

No logging for container execution

**Describe the results you expected:**

Container execution should be logged so that it is possible to manual run the container and see why the container failed and to produce better bug reports.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 2.0.0-dev
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


```
(paste your output here)
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: This seems like a request for the OCI runtime (runc/crun) - we don't directly run the process, instead we run Conmon, which then runs the OCI runtime, which performs the exec. We do print information on how Conmon was called.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I think @mheon answered this query, reopen if You believe we need to reveal more information.
